name: vornik-companion
description: Async-offload companion for Claude Code with project RAG memory + per-repo scoping (migration 75). Delegate reviews / audits / validations / research / bulk-ingest; recall + remember scoped to the current repo so one project's RAG can serve multiple repos without cross-pollution. SessionStart digest surfaces completed delegations and recent memory updates. /upload attaches local files to delegations as base64 INPUT artifacts without burning client tokens; /rag-ingest (0.4.2) is the dedicated bulk-ingest entry point for project RAG memory (supports directories + --tag). 0.4.5: hotfix — session-start.sh now emits the v2-style {hookSpecificOutput,hookEventName,additionalContext} JSON envelope so the digest renders in the Claude Code v2.1+ welcome banner. Pre-0.4.5 raw markdown reached the model's context but was silently dropped from the banner. 0.4.4 wired hooks.json; 0.4.5 makes the wired script speak the v2 protocol. 0.4.6: /rag-ingest gained an explicit per-file size cap (--max-file-bytes, default 512 KiB) + accurate docs after the companion-rag-ingest WORKFLOW was fixed to copy staged sources with run_shell (cp/cat) instead of re-emitting bytes via file_read→file_write — the latter silently failed on large files (260 KiB CHANGELOG). Companion swarm template now also ships the rag-ingester role. 0.4.7: repo-scope resolvers (session-start.sh + /upload + /rag-ingest) now fall back to the current folder basename when there's no git origin / not a git repo, instead of returning empty — an empty scope surfaced to the operator as context "none" and silently routed recall/remember/delegate project-wide. All three resolvers stay in lockstep. 0.4.8: delegate guidance hardened after the 2026-06-05 silent-skip incident — file paths in a delegate prompt are never uploaded; daemon-side require_input_artifacts now rejects artifact-less rag-ingest delegations, and the delegate command/skill docs steer to /rag-ingest + /upload. 0.4.9: /rag-ingest docs updated for the 2026-06-11 server-side rewrite — companion-rag-ingest is now DETERMINISTIC and agent-free: the workflow has no agent step and the executor ingests staged input artifacts directly by ID (ingest_input_artifacts), removing the weak rag-ingester LLM from the copy loop that repeatedly claimed produced_files it never wrote (the 'try a different tool' large-file ingest failures). File size and model quality no longer affect whether the copy lands. 0.4.10: companion-architectural-review workflow now reviews the STAGED input artifact as its source of truth and refuses to review project memory (RAG holds stale/multiple doc revisions → contradictory findings); /upload is documented as workflow-generic and the canonical doc-review path (`/upload companion-architectural-review "…" <file>`); the delegate skill + /review + /delegate docs steer clients to attach files via /upload for any file-bearing workflow rather than naming a path in a delegate prompt the agent container can't read. 0.5.0: BREAKING command rename — dropped the redundant vornik- prefix from plugin actions (/vornik-upload→/upload, /vornik-rag-ingest→/rag-ingest, skill vornik-delegate→delegate); the plugin namespace already conveys vornik-companion, so vornik-companion:vornik-upload was doubly redundant. Usage guidance strengthened to make companion-architectural-review the default MERGE GATE for non-trivial changes and design docs: commit, upload the diff (git diff base...branch > file) or design doc as a staged artifact via /upload, block on the verdict, fix every Critical/Important finding, re-review, then merge; single-quote /upload prompts with NO double-quotes or backticks (they break the bash wrapper); delegate long reviews/audits/research proactively at task start. 0.6.0: new /memory-correct command + memory_correct MCP tool — soft-refute a wrong/stale RAG fact (top hybrid-search matches flip to validation_status='refuted' and drop out of recall) and optionally store the correction as a verified chunk; the programmatic equivalent of the dispatcher's chat-side memory_correct, so a host LLM can make newer findings take precedence over superseded notes (requires a memory_write-scoped key). ALSO: /remember rewritten to the heredoc→file→json.dumps→POST pattern (same hardening /upload and /rag-ingest already use) so long notes with quotes/backticks/newlines/em-dashes land intact instead of being mangled or truncated by inline MCP tool-call serialization; /remember and /memory-correct both gained leading --scope/--class/--ttl (remember) and --max/--scope (memory-correct) flags. 0.7.0: /memory-correct + the memory_correct MCP tool gain a surgical by-id mode — pass one or more --chunk-id (chunk_ids[] over MCP, from a prior /recall) to refute exactly those chunks instead of the claim-search top matches. Needed because once an authoritative correction exists it out-ranks the stale chunk in a claim search, so claim-mode would demote the correction rather than the stale note; by-id targets the right chunk. wrong_claim and chunk_ids are now either-or.
